wq.aqi.title: Air Quality Index
wq.aqi.advice_good: Air quality is satisfactory. Enjoy outdoor activities.
wq.aqi.advice_fair: Acceptable air quality. Sensitive individuals should limit prolonged outdoor exertion.
wq.aqi.advice_moderate: Unhealthy for sensitive groups. Consider reducing outdoor time.
wq.aqi.advice_poor: Unhealthy. Avoid prolonged outdoor activities, especially for children and elderly.
wq.aqi.advice_very_poor: Very unhealthy. Stay indoors. Wear N95 mask if going outside.
wq.aqi.advice_hazardous: Hazardous. Health emergency. Avoid all outdoor activities.

wq.feature.accurate_desc: Powered by ECMWF and GFS global models — the same data used by professional meteorologists.
wq.feature.fast_desc: Results in under 1 second. No server round-trip — weather data loaded directly from Open-Meteo CDN.
wq.feature.free_desc: No registration, no API key, no usage limits. Open-Meteo is a non-commercial, open-source API.
wq.faq.section_subtitle: Common questions about the real-time global weather query tool
wq.faq.q1: Is this weather tool completely free?
wq.faq.a1: Yes, ToolboxNova's weather query tool is completely free. It uses the Open-Meteo public API with no API key, no account registration, and no usage limits.
wq.faq.q2: How accurate are the weather forecasts?
wq.faq.a2: We use ECMWF and GFS global meteorological model data, updated every 1-6 hours. Accuracy is highest within 48 hours; 7-day forecasts are reliable for planning, and 14-day forecasts indicate general trends.
wq.faq.q3: What air quality data is provided?
wq.faq.a3: We display the US AQI index (0-300+) and real-time concentrations of PM2.5, PM10, O₃ (ozone), NO₂, SO₂, and CO. Data comes from the Copernicus CAMS atmospheric monitoring model.
wq.faq.q4: Can I check historical weather data?
wq.faq.a4: Yes. Switch to the '30-Day History' tab to view daily high/low temperatures and precipitation for the past month, retrieved free via the Open-Meteo Archive API.
wq.faq.q5: How do I share weather information?
wq.faq.a5: Click the 'Save Weather Snapshot' button to generate a beautifully formatted weather card image, ready to download or share on social media platforms.
wq.faq.q6: How often is the data updated?
wq.faq.a6: Current conditions refresh every 15 minutes; forecast data updates every 1-6 hours. The 'Updated at' timestamp in the metrics grid shows the exact last refresh time.
wq.faq.q7: Which languages can I use to search for cities?
wq.faq.a7: The search box accepts city names in Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, and many more languages. The geocoding engine matches against a database of 3 million+ cities worldwide.
wq.faq.q8: How do I use the weather map?
wq.faq.a8: In the Weather Map panel, choose from five layers: Temperature, Precipitation, Wind Speed, Cloud Cover, and Pressure. The map uses real-time OpenWeatherMap satellite tiles and is fully zoomable.
wq.faq.q9: Can I change the temperature unit?
wq.faq.a9: Yes. Use the °C / °F toggle buttons at the top of the current weather card. Wind speed can be switched between km/h, mph, and m/s. Your preferences are automatically saved locally.
wq.faq.q10: What does the UV Index mean?
wq.faq.a10: The UV Index measures the intensity of solar ultraviolet radiation: 0-2 Low (no protection needed), 3-5 Moderate (apply sunscreen), 6-7 High (sunscreen + hat required), 8-10 Very High (avoid midday sun), 11+ Extreme (stay indoors). It typically peaks around solar noon on clear summer days.
wq.home.desc: Real-time global weather · 14-day forecast · AQI · Wind maps
wq.world_map.title: Explore Weather Anywhere on the Globe
wq.world_map.subtitle: Click anywhere on the map to instantly get real-time weather for that location
wq.world_map.click_hint: Click on the map to view weather
wq.content.section1_body: Weather is the state of the atmosphere at a particular time and place — a complex interplay of temperature, humidity, pressure, wind, and precipitation. Unlike climate, which describes long-term patterns, weather can change dramatically within hours. Modern meteorological forecasting leverages satellite imagery, Doppler radar, weather balloons, and supercomputer-driven models like ECMWF and GFS to make predictions with remarkable accuracy. With our real-time weather tool, you get the same data that professional forecasters rely on, delivered directly to your browser.
wq.content.section2_body: Today's forecasts begin with global observing networks — over 40,000 weather stations, thousands of weather balloons launched twice daily, ocean buoys, and a fleet of polar-orbiting satellites. This raw data feeds into Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) models that divide the atmosphere into three-dimensional grids and solve equations of fluid dynamics, thermodynamics, and radiation physics. The European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and the US Global Forecast System (GFS) are among the most widely trusted. Our tool uses Open-Meteo, which seamlessly blends these model outputs for any coordinate on Earth.
wq.content.section3_body: The Air Quality Index (AQI) tells you how clean or polluted the outdoor air is and what associated health effects might be a concern. Our tool shows the US AQI scale (0–300+) along with concentrations of six key pollutants: fine particulate matter (PM2.5 and PM10), ground-level ozone (O₃), nitrogen dioxide (NO₂), sulfur dioxide (SO₂), and carbon monoxide (CO). Data comes from the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS), a European initiative providing continuous global atmospheric composition data.
wq.content.section4_title: Reading the Wind: Beaufort Scale & Beyond
wq.content.section4_body: Wind speed is measured using the Beaufort scale (0–12), anemometers, and modern ultrasonic sensors. At the surface, wind direction profoundly influences local temperature — a northerly wind in winter brings Arctic air, while a southerly wind in summer can carry tropical moisture. Wind shear, the change of wind speed or direction with altitude, is a critical factor in thunderstorm development and aviation safety. Our weather map's wind layer visualizes surface wind patterns globally, updated every hour.
wq.content.section5_body: Rain, snow, sleet, and hail all begin high in clouds as tiny ice crystals or water droplets that collide and grow until gravity pulls them earthward. The type of precipitation that reaches the surface depends on the temperature profile through which it falls. A thin warm layer above freezing and cold air at the surface creates freezing rain — one of winter's most hazardous weather events. Our 14-day forecast shows daily precipitation probability and expected totals, so you can plan outdoor activities with confidence.
wq.content.section6_title: UV Index: Protecting Yourself from the Sun
wq.content.section6_body: The UV Index (UVI) is an international standard measurement of the strength of ultraviolet radiation from the Sun at a particular place and time. UV radiation is the primary cause of sunburn, premature skin aging, and skin cancer. The index runs from 0 (no risk at night) to 11+ (extreme risk during a clear summer midday). At UVI 3 or above, the WHO recommends applying broad-spectrum SPF 30+ sunscreen, wearing protective clothing, and seeking shade during midday hours (10am–4pm solar time).
